evals.prompt: Let's pick this up — I want to start on the scope-check primitive design bead. Run /recall before I claim it.
evals.rationale: Tests bead-id input, default-on trigger #1 (picking up new bead), phrase trigger 'let's pick this up', broad multi-tier surface (L3/L2B/L2A/skills), L1 skip-default, brain-run discipline. Particularly probes content-extractor delegation on large `bd show` persisted-output and load-bearing-surprise prelude when the bead turns out to be closed.
assertions: Agent invokes `bd show` on the scope-check primitive design bead (or equivalent) to frame the query
Agent dispatches `content-extractor` (NOT raw `Read`) on any `bd show` persisted-output file >10KB
Agent queries L2B via `bd memories` with relevant keywords
Agent queries L2A via `cm context`
Agent surfaces a `Load-bearing surprise` prelude when the bead is closed (state mismatch with user framing)
Agent does NOT invoke `cass` (L1 skipped by default — no retro/audit phrase trigger present)
Agent reads cited ADR-Dk sections before applying firmness labels (no reasoned-from-context [FIRM] labels)
Output contains the 5 canonical sections (FIRM constraints / Relevant prior context / Skills in scope / Verification needed / Empty signals)

evals.prompt: Where were we on the memory-layer architecture? I want to brainstorm an extension to L2A retention policies. Run /recall before /brainstorm.
evals.rationale: Tests free-text input (no bead-id), scope-shift trigger, pre-brainstorm default-on trigger, phrase trigger 'where were we'. Validates that volume-delegation rules don't spuriously fire when reads stay under threshold. Validates firmness-verification rule against ADR-013 (which has mixed firmness: D1/D3 FIRM, D2/D4 FLEXIBLE, D8 EXPLORATORY).
assertions: Agent frames query as 'L2A retention policy extension' (or equivalent topic-anchored framing)
Agent reads ADR-013 directly (not via subagent) and labels firmness correctly: D1 FIRM, D2 FLEXIBLE, D3 FIRM, D4 FLEXIBLE, D8 EXPLORATORY
Agent queries L2B via `bd memories memory-layer` (or L2A retention keywords)
Agent queries L2A via `cm context`
Agent does NOT dispatch file-scanner or content-extractor (volume threshold not crossed)
Agent does NOT invoke `cass` (no retro/audit trigger)
Agent surfaces `/brainstorm`, `/adr-write`, and `/compound` in Skills in scope (composition handoffs)
Output contains all 5 canonical sections with non-empty Empty signals (probing for absences)

evals.prompt: Remind me what we decided about haiku-tier subagent model selection — I want to audit our reasoning before scaling it to other agents.
evals.rationale: Tests L1 explicit-retro invoke branch — textbook trigger combining literal phrase 'remind me what we decided about' (from skill's phrase-triggers list) with 'audit' semantic. Iter 3 of initial soldier-proof run MISSED this trigger; skill text was strengthened post-loop. This eval validates the fix.
assertions: Agent invokes `cass search` (or `cass view`) on relevant topic keywords — L1 explicitly engaged per retro/audit trigger
Agent recognizes `cm context` (L2A) is NOT a substitute for `cass` (L1) — both are queried where relevant
Agent surfaces a CASS-derived reasoning trail (prior session excerpts) in the orientation block, not just current-state artifacts
Agent's absences probe correctly identifies whether the decision is canonicalized at L3 (ADR) or only at lower tiers
Agent reads agent frontmatter files directly when citing specific tier assignments
Output contains all 5 canonical sections; FIRM constraints may be empty if no ADR encodes the decision
